qa_pairs.question: Who has access to a Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: All of a PI's activated sponsored user roles are always members of the PI's default RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Can membership in a Default RAP be modified?
qa_pairs.answer: No, membership in a PI's default RAP cannot be modified. However, a PI can at any time deactivate any role they sponsor.

qa_pairs.question: Who is automatically added to a RAC RAP with HPC resources?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new RAP is created with HPC resources (e.g., CPU, GPU, project storage, nearline storage, etc.), CCDB automatically adds as members of the RAP a) all of a PI's sponsored user roles, b) all associated Co-PI roles, and c) all sponsored user roles of all of the associated Co-PIs. Any new role that the PI sponsors after a RAP has been created will also be automatically added as a member of the RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Who is initially added as a member to a RAC RAP with Cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: At the time a new RAP is created with Cloud resources allocated, only the PI is added as a member of that RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Can a PI modify the membership of their RAC RAPs?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the PI can modify the membership of any of their RAC RAPs at any time. Any user with an active Alliance account can be added as a member of a RAC RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a PI modify RAC RAP membership?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI may want to allow access to user roles they are not sponsoring (e.g., a Co-PI) or remove one or more of their sponsored user roles from their RAC RAP and limit them to only be able to access their default RAP.

qa_pairs.question: What are the different RAP membership permission levels?
qa_pairs.answer: There are three RAP membership permission levels: Owner, Manager, and Member.

qa_pairs.question: What are the responsibilities and limitations of a RAP Owner?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI is the sole owner of the RAP and all the allocations associated to that RAP, and this cannot be changed. The owner can add or remove RAP managers and members.

qa_pairs.question: What can a RAP Manager do?
qa_pairs.answer: A RAP Manager has elevated permission (on CCDB only, not on the clusters) delegated by the owner or another manager that allows making membership changes. Managers can also use the PI's allocation in the corresponding cluster(s).

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations for a RAP Manager regarding file systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Members that have been promoted to Manager on a RAC RAP cannot make changes (e.g., change ownership of the files, apply ACLs, manage permissions on Globus, etc.) on the project/nearline file systems on behalf of the PIs.

qa_pairs.question: What can a RAP Member do?
qa_pairs.answer: Members can use the PI's allocation in the corresponding cluster(s). Members cannot make any modifications to the RAP membership.

qa_pairs.question: How is RAP membership represented and what does it define?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership is represented as a group in LDAP. It defines a group of users that are authorized to submit jobs against the RAPI (which is the ID of the RAP) and share files within the same Unix group.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find detailed instructions on adding members to a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: For detailed instructions about how to add members to a RAC RAP, please visit the Using a resource allocation page.

qa_pairs.question: Can a person have more than one Alliance account or CCI?
qa_pairs.answer: No, each person can only have one Alliance account, meaning one unique personal and national identifier (CCI). Requests for duplicate CCIs are refused.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have an existing Alliance account but my position or sponsor has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have an existing CCI and have changed position, you should apply for a new role (which will have a new CCRI) instead. To do so, log in with your existing account and visit the 'apply for a new role' form on the CCDB portal.

qa_pairs.question: How can I reset my password if I've forgotten it?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have forgotten your password, you may reset it via the provided link on the CCDB portal.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I can no longer access the email address on file for my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: If you can no longer access the email address you have on file, please email accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca and they can update it for you.

qa_pairs.question: How do I sign up for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to the register link on the CCDB portal to sign up.

qa_pairs.question: What must I do before I can get an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: You will have to accept certain policies and agreements to get an account.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I read the policies and agreements for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: You can read the policies on the Alliance website.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I view the agreements I've accepted for my Alliance account after it's approved?
qa_pairs.answer: Once your account has been approved, you will be able to see at any time on CCDB the agreements that you have accepted by going to My Account --> Agreements.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main types of roles when applying for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: There are two main types of roles: sponsor roles, often referred to as Primary Investigators or PIs, and sponsored roles.
